edges.description: Impaired triacylglycerol hydrolysis causes lipid-droplet accumulation in muscle.

nodes.description: Undegraded triacylglycerol accumulates as cytoplasmic lipid droplets in skeletal and cardiac muscle fibers (and leukocytes, producing Jordans' anomaly), driving myopathy and cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
NLSDM usually presents skeletal
myopathy, cardiomyopathy and the multiple organs dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
A total of 23
mutations were identified including 11 (47.8%) point mutations, eight (34.8%)
deletions

nodes.description: Biallelic PNPLA2 variants reduce adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL), the rate-limiting enzyme for intracellular triacylglycerol hydrolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy (NLSDM) is a rare
clinical heterogeneous disorder caused by mutations in the patatin-like
phospholipase domain-containing 2 (PNPLA2) gene
